data.title: Ladder Capital Corp Reports Results for the Quarter Ended June 30, 2026
data.description: In New York, Ladder Capital Corp (NYSE: LADR) reported its operating performance for the quarter ending on June 30, 2026. During this period, the company generated a GAAP income before taxes of $16.3 million, equivalent to $0.12 per diluted earnings per share (“EPS”). Additionally, distributable earnings reached $30.8 million, translating to $0.24 per distributable EPS. The company expressed satisfaction with its performance, highlighting growth in its loan portfolio and distributable earnings in the second quarter.

data.title: Varcity Launches the Retirement Renaissance with First University Community at Purdue
data.description: Varcity has recently closed the development of Varcity at Purdue in West Lafayette, Indiana. This retirement community marks the company's first university-based project and signifies a larger effort to transform the concept of retirement nationwide. In collaboration with Purdue University and the Purdue for Life Foundation, Varcity at Purdue aims to introduce a novel approach to retirement living. Set to welcome residents in Summer 2028 on the Purdue University campus, this initiative targets individuals who view retirement as a new chapter in life.

data.title: Global Payments to Report Second Quarter Results on August 5, 2026
data.description: Atlanta-based Global Payments Inc. (NYSE: GPN), a major player in payment technology and software for businesses globally, is set to announce its second-quarter financial results on August 5, 2026, prior to market opening. Following the release, a live audio webcast to review the results will be held at 8:00 a.m. ET. To participate, individuals can tune into the webcast through the investor relations section of the company's website at investors.globalpayments.com. Those unable to attend can access a replay of the audio webcast later.

data.title: From Bust to Boom: San Francisco Home Prices Are Now $1 Million Higher Than Housing-Crash Bottom
data.description: In Seattle, a recent report from Redfin revealed that the average price of a home in San Francisco has increased by over $1 million since hitting its lowest point during the financial crisis. The city's housing market has proven to be remarkably strong, largely due to the influence of the thriving tech sector and its position as a hotbed for advancements in artificial intelligence technology. The median sale price in the San Francisco metropolitan area experienced a decline before rebounding strongly.

data.title: Prosper Medical Raises $16M to Bring AI-powered Concierge Care to All
data.description: A San Francisco-based company, Prosper Medical, has recently secured $16 million in funding to expand its AI-driven concierge primary care platform. Co-founded by Ryan McQuaid and Dr. James Wantuck, the duo previously developed and sold PlushCare, a direct-to-consumer telehealth platform, for $450 million. Leveraging AI technology, Prosper Medical aims to tackle the ongoing issue in healthcare of providing tailored and efficient primary care services on a large scale. The company's innovative approach sets the stage for addressing the challenges in delivering personalized medical care effectively.

data.title: Empery Digital Announces $20 Million Strategic Investment in Cardinal Data Power
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Empery Digital Inc. (NASDAQ: EMPD), known for its bitcoin treasury strategy, revealed that it completed a $20 million preferred equity investment on July 20, 2026. This investment secured an approximately 8% ownership in Cardinal Data Power, Inc. (CDP), a Hunt Properties affiliate specializing in powered data center campuses, notably behind-the-meter projects. This move marks a significant step for Empery Digital in diversifying its portfolio and expanding its presence in the digital infrastructure sector.

data.title: Bitget obtains license for expansion in New Zealand, registered as financial service provider with regulator. #crypto #exchange #NewZealand
data.description: Bitget, a cryptocurrency exchange, recently announced its registration as a financial service provider with the financial regulator of New Zealand. This move allows Bitget to broaden its services within the country.

data.title: UN warns: Victims lose up to $114B yearly due to Southeast Asian scam networks, now united and tech-savvy, favoring crypto.
data.description: The UN Office on Drugs and Crime has issued a warning about the merging of previously disjointed criminal groups into a unified, technology-driven network. This integrated criminal economy is now heavily reliant on cryptocurrencies for its operations. This shift towards a singular syndicate has raised concerns within the UNODC due to the increased efficiency and complexity of criminal activities.

data.title: Vital Farms to Report Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results on August 6, 2026
data.description: Announcement from Vital Farms, a company listed on Nasdaq as VITL, operating as a Certified B Corporation in the ethically produced foods sector. They are set to disclose their financial performance for the second quarter ending June 28, 2026, on August 6, 2026, before the market opens. The presentation will be followed by a conference call and webcast at 8:30 a.m. ET on the same day to review these results. Interested individuals can join the call by registering in advance for dial-in information.

data.title: XPEL, Inc. to Host Conference Call to Discuss Second Quarter 2026 Results
data.description: XPEL, Inc. (Nasdaq: XPEL), a worldwide leader in protective films and coatings, will hold a conference call and webcast on Wednesday, August 5, 2026, at 8:30 a.m. EST to review the Company’s performance in the second quarter of 2026. For the live webcast, visit XPEL, Inc.'s website at https://investor.xpel.com/events-and-presentations. To join the call by phone, dial (888) 506-0062 about five minutes before the scheduled time.
